{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks for the organism that causes bacterial endocarditis. From the retrieved documents, multiple sources confirm the etiology of bacterial endocarditis. Document [18] states that most cases are caused by viridans streptococci, Streptococcus gallolyticus, Staphylococcus aureus, coagulase-negative staphylococci, HACEK organisms, and enterococci. Specifically, Document [1] and Document [8] emphasize that penicillin-sensitive Streptococcus viridans is the predominant cause of subacute bacterial endocarditis. Document [24] further specifies that viridans streptococci and S. gallolyticus are common causes, with treatment regimens based on this pathogen. Streptococcus sanguis is mentioned as a member of the viridans group (Document [14]), and since viridans group streptococci includes S. sanguis and S. mitis, S. salivarius, S. anginosus, and others, it is part of the broader group. However, the most specific and directly referenced organism as the predominant cause is Streptococcus viridans. Option D, Streptococcus pyogenes (Group A strep), is associated with other infections like pharyngitis and skin infections, but not primarily with endocarditis. Option A, Lactobacillus, is not mentioned in any of the documents as a cause. Therefore, the best-supported answer is Streptococcus viridans.", "answer_choice": "C"}
